generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance Motor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "A permanent magnet synchronous motor that converts electrical current (I_M) into rotational torque (τ_M) for the first axis of MABI MAX 100 industrial robot"
]

instance GearWheel : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "A mechanical component in the gear transmission system that transmits torque from the motor to the cycloidal gearbox"
]

instance CycloidalGearbox : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "A multi-stage cycloidal gearbox system that reduces torque (τ_G → τ_out) and adjusts rotational speed for the robot's first axis"
]

instance SINUMERIK840DController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller that processes position, speed, and acceleration commands and generates motor current inputs (I_M) for real-time control"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring motor electrical current (I_M) transmitted via MQTT for friction modeling"
]

instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder measuring motor angular position for real-time data streaming"
]

instance SecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder measuring output angular position of the gear system for backlash detection"
]

instance OutputAngleSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Direct angular position sensor measuring robot's first axis output angle"
]

instance MotorCurrentStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
    base:desc "Real-time motor current (I_M) data streamed via MQTT protocol to digital shadow for friction modeling"
]

instance EncoderPositionStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder, SecondaryEncoder
    base:desc "Real-time angular position data streamed via MQTT protocol for encoder-based friction detection"
]

instance DerivedTorqueVelocityStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Derived torque (τ_M, τ_G, τ_out) and angular velocity (ω) data computed from manufacturer dynamics models"
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance MotorGearboxBDD : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram (BDD) describing the first axis as composition of Motor, GearWheel, and CycloidalGearbox components with rotational (torque/velocity) and electrical (current) flows"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLTool
]

instance MotorGearboxIBD : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram (IBD) detailing internal structure of the gear transmission system"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLTool
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box analytical friction model implementing Coulomb-Viscous friction characteristics (Coulomb + viscous components) in MATLAB Simulink"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulink
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box analytical friction model implementing Stribeck friction characteristics (temperature-dependent viscous friction) in MATLAB Simulink"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulink
]

instance LuGreFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box analytical friction model implementing LuGre friction characteristics (stiction, Coulomb, viscous) in MATLAB Simulink"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulink
]

instance BlackBoxFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Black-box data-driven friction model trained on historical operational data for dynamic friction estimation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulink
]

instance MeasuredMotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentStream
    base:desc "Raw motor current (I_M) measurements from physical sensors for friction analysis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulink
]

instance MeasuredPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    DTDFVocab:fromData EncoderPositionStream
    base:desc "Raw angular position data from encoders for encoder-based friction detection"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulink
]

instance DerivedTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    DTDFVocab:fromData DerivedTorqueVelocityStream
    base:desc "Torque (τ_M, τ_G, τ_out) and angular velocity (ω) data derived from manufacturer dynamics models"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulink
]

instance SimulatedFrictionOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Outputs from friction models (simulated motor/gearbox outputs) subtracted from reference data to isolate friction losses"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulink
]

instance FrictionLossData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Isolated friction losses calculated by comparing measured and simulated outputs"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationDashboard
]

instance ModelParametersMetadata : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Source tracing metadata including MQTT channel IDs, sensor IDs, and gear ratios for traceability"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulink
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML modeling tool used for creating structural (BDD/IBD) and behavioral models of the gear transmission system"
    DTDFVocab:enables MotorGearboxBDD, MotorGearboxIBD
]

instance MATLABSimulink : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks MATLAB Simulink environment for executable friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, LuGre) and system dynamics simulations"
    DTDFVocab:enables CoulombViscousFrictionModel, StribeckFrictionModel, LuGreFrictionModel, BlackBoxFrictionModel
]

instance MQTTProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol for real-time sensor data streaming (motor current, encoder positions) to digital shadow"
    DTDFVocab:enables MotorCurrentStream, EncoderPositionStream
]

instance CustomDashboard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom visualization dashboard integrating data traces (motor current, torque, angular velocity) and friction model outputs"
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationDashboard
]

// C6: Services
instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time identification and quantification of static, kinetic, and viscous friction in the gearbox using white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) models"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionModelParametersInsight, FrictionVisualizationInsight
]

instance ModelRefinementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous updating of friction models via live data comparison between measured and simulated outputs"
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelParameterUpdateAction, ControlStrategyOptimizationAction
]

instance VisualizationDashboard : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Unified visualization of data traces (motor current, torque, angular velocity) and friction model outputs for operational insights"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionDeviationAlert
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionModelParametersInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of friction model parameters based on live data to improve motion accuracy"
]

instance FrictionVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visual representation of friction characteristics (static, kinetic, viscous) for operational monitoring"
]

instance ModelParameterUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic parameter adjustment of friction models based on real-time data comparison"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ControlStrategyOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Optimization of real-time control algorithms using friction modeling insights"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FrictionDeviationAlert : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Visual flagging of deviations between simulated and measured data prompting maintenance or tooling changes"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C1: Environment
instance RobotFirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains Motor, GearWheel, CycloidalGearbox, SINUMERIK840DController,
        MotorCurrentSensor, MotorEncoder, SecondaryEncoder, OutputAngleSensor
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twinning time scale is **real-time and near-real-time**:
  - **Data Acquisition Frequency**: Sensor data (e.g., motor current, encoders) is streamed at **high frequencies** (implied by live operational context, though exact frequency not specified).
  - **Model Update Rate**: Friction models are dynamically refined during operation, with **continuous identification** of friction losses via live data comparison (e.g., simulated vs. measured torque/velocity).
  - **Dashboard Synchronization**: Visualization updates are synchronized with operational data streams to provide **real-time feedback** for control adjustments."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is **purpose-driven and modular**:
  - **Core Components**:
    - **Digital Shadow (DS)**: A **passive real-time representation** of the robot’s first axis, linking live measurements (e.g., encoders, current) with friction models via **SysML and Simulink**.
    - **Processing Layer**: **Simulation models** (e.g., Motor Simulation, Gearbox Simulation) compute friction-included outputs for comparison with measured data.
    - **Dashboard**: Aggregates data traces (I_M, τ, ω) and model outputs for visualization and decision-making.
  - **Interactions**: The DS acts as a **foundational baseline** for a broader **Digital Twin** of the robotic system, enabling integration with higher-level optimization or task-planning services."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin spans the following lifecycle stages:
  1. **As Designed** – Defined via **SysML Block Definition Diagrams (BDD)** and **Internal Block Diagrams (IBD)** for structural modeling.
  2. **As Operated** – Enables real-time friction analysis and model validation during robot operation.
  *(Note: The document does not explicitly mention 'As Manufactured' as a distinct lifecycle phase, though SysML is used for development.)"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  - **Model Validation**: Friction models (e.g., LuGre, Dahl) are validated against **live measurements** (torque/velocity differences between simulated and measured outputs).
  - **Uncertainty Handling**: Backlash and elasticity are mitigated via **encoder differences** (motor vs. secondary encoder).
  - **Verification**: Traceability via **DSRM** ensures that all data points originate from validated sources (e.g., MQTT streams, Simulink simulations).
  - **Parameter Calibration**: Manufacturer-provided dynamics models (e.g., robot-specific parameters) are used to derive accurate torque/velocity estimates."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT technical connection uses:
  - **Data Transmission**: **MQTT** protocol for real-time streaming of sensor data (I_M, encoders).
  - **Model Integration**: **MathWorks MATLAB Simulink** for executable friction models, linked to the digital shadow via **processing components**.
  - **Visualization**: Custom dashboard integrating data traces and model outputs (e.g., torque/velocity comparisons)."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DT incorporates the following standards:
  - **Modeling**: **SysML** for structural/behavioral modeling.
  - **Simulation**: **MathWorks MATLAB Simulink** for executable models.
  - **Data Exchange**: **MQTT** for real-time sensor data streaming.
  - **Digital Shadow Framework**: Aligns with the **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)** from Kritzinger et al. [32] and Michael et al. [15], including **Engineering Models**, **Data Calculation Models**, and **Simulation Models**."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety considerations include:
  1. **Operational Safety** – The robot’s **safety cell** restricts the working range to [-90°°, 90°] and limits maximum speed to **75°/s** to prevent accidents.
  2. **Passive Digital Shadow** – Functions as a **one-way data flow** (Kritzinger et al.), avoiding autonomous control or remote execution risks.
  *(Note: The document does not address MQTT security protocols or remote operation safety explicitly.)"
]
